summary: TEST A (ADD): local persistence PASS (ZTest1784675XXX added, survives parent reload). Manager-side propagation NOT observed in this session because Supabase push failed (see rca). TEST B (DELETE just-added child): PASS on parent (reload keeps kids=[amu,john,QAKid]) AND on manager (Members page: Showing 3 of 3, ZTest absent). TEST C (DELETE pre-existing QAKid1784673895): FAIL — parent local shows [amu,john] immediately after Save (delete applied), but after reload QAKid COMES BACK to [amu,john,QAKid]. Manager Members likewise still shows QAKid (Total Members=3). Regression PASS — parent Notifications shows only real items ('Membership payment due for July 2026' + 'Welcome to the new season!' 1 Sept 2025) with NO banned placeholder strings ('Neptune BC', 'fundraising event on 25 February', 'Tuesday training has been moved', 'monthly membership payment of €50'). Manager Dashboard still renders (Total Members=3, teams=5, recent activity intact). Debounce fix helps for intra-session state churn but delete of a REMOTE-PERSISTED child does NOT survive reload when the Supabase contribution push fails.

ui_bugs.issue: Deleting a PRE-EXISTING remote-persisted child does not survive a parent reload. Save applies locally (dlbc_players parent kids = [amu,john]) but on reload the deleted child reappears (kids=[amu,john,QAKid1784673895]). Console consistently shows '[app_state] member contribution sync failed TypeError: Failed to fetch' — the Supabase pushMemberRosterContribution is failing intermittently. Because remote Supabase still holds the stale contribution (or auth.user_metadata still lists the child), pullRemoteAppState + mergeContributionIntoPlayers OR syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata rehydrates the deleted child on next mount. TEST B (delete a just-added child) accidentally passes only because that child was never successfully pushed to Supabase in the first place (both add and delete pushes fail identically, so remote never had it — local trivially wins).

Silent failure of the Supabase contribution push (Failed to fetch, likely DNS/CORS/network). The 250ms debounce reduces the race but does nothing when the network call ultimately fails. There is no retry queue / no per-user client timestamp, so when the push fails the app falls back to trusting stale remote on reload.

integration_issues.flow: Parent deletes a pre-existing registered child via Profile > Registered children > Remove > Save children -> manager Members list should drop the row
integration_issues.issue: Delete of a remote-persisted child does not propagate. Parent reload shows the child returning; manager Members page therefore continues to show 3 members including QAKid1784673895 (Total Members=3, 'Showing 3 of 3 members').

In this session, adding ZTest<epoch> did not appear on the manager Members page (Total Members remained 3 with amu/john/QAKid, ZTest absent) — the Supabase contribution push failed for the add too. iteration_3 previously verified add propagation; the current failure is likely due to intermittent Supabase network from the browser.

action_items: P0: Make delete durable across reload even when Supabase push fails. Concretely: (a) in ProfileTab.handleSaveChildren, always call supabase.auth.updateUser({data:{registeredChildren: next}}) on BOTH add and delete BEFORE returning, so auth.user_metadata (which syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata reads) is authoritative for own children; (b) attach a monotonically increasing client-side updated_at (Date.now()) to the parent Player row on every save, and change syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata + mergeContributionIntoPlayers so that a fresher local timestamp beats stale remote — do not restore auth-metadata children when local was written more recently; (c) queue and retry pushMemberRosterContribution on network failure (with exponential backoff) instead of dropping it silently.

P1: Investigate why 'TypeError: Failed to fetch' happens for Supabase from the browser in this preview env. If CORS/DNS is fine intermittently, add per-request retry + telemetry so we can distinguish 'push failed' from 'push succeeded but overwritten'.

P2: Clean-up — parent's live dlbc_players still contains QAKid1784673895 (delete didn't persist). Once the fix lands, the test parent should be able to Remove + Save and see the roster shrink to [amu, john]. Currently baseline is 3 kids not the '2 kids' the review request claimed.

P3: src/pages/PlayerDashboard.tsx (2000+ lines) and src/lib/clubData.ts (2400+ lines) still exceed the 700-line guideline; splitting ProfileTab and the sync layer would make these reload/race conditions much easier to reason about.
critical_code_review_comments: The debounce (scheduleMemberContributionPush 250ms) correctly serialises the intra-session push order, but does NOT solve the underlying reload-from-stale-source-of-truth problem. As soon as the network push fails once (Failed to fetch was observed on every save in this session), the delete becomes invisible to Supabase and to any client that reloads. There must be either (a) a durable retry queue, (b) local-wins-with-timestamp on reload merge, or (c) a synchronous auth.updateUser call that shrinks auth.user_metadata.registeredChildren (Supabase auth is more reachable than the app_state table in most cases and is what syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata already prefers).

The rule 'syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata only restores when LOCAL is empty' is still fragile because on cold reload there is a brief window where local is truly empty (before rehydration) and remote-derived merges can slip in. Even with the debounce, the reload path pulls from Supabase before local has been fully constructed with the parent's row.

The apparent TEST B success is misleading — it succeeds because both the add push AND the delete push fail (Failed to fetch), so remote never diverges from the pre-add baseline. If Supabase were working, the add would push [amu,john,QAKid,ZTest]; the delete would then need to push [amu,john,QAKid] to overwrite it. The current fix likely works in that happy path (because the debounce fires the LAST push), but the FAILURE path (push errors) is unhandled.

Notifications implementation continues to be clean: only 'Membership payment due for July 2026' and the real 'Welcome to the new season!' announcement (dated 1 Sept 2025) are shown. None of the banned placeholder strings appear. Read/Delete controls render correctly. Manager Dashboard 'Recent activity' correctly shows real seeded fixtures (e.g., 'Match scheduled: vs Neptune BC'), which is a legitimate match row, not a leftover notification placeholder.

success_rate.frontend: 50% — TEST A local-persist pass but add propagation to manager not observed; TEST B pass (on both sides, but see caveat); TEST C fail (delete of pre-existing child reverts on reload and stays on manager); notifications regression pass.

test_credentials: Parent: cooler74.ea@gmail.com / test12 — CURRENT baseline is 3 registered children (amu, john, QAKid1784673895); after this run the baseline is unchanged because TEST C's delete of QAKid did not persist. Manager: manager@dublinlions.ie / lions2025.
seed_data_creation: Created child ZTest1784674993 (dob 2012-05-15, Male) during TEST A. Deleted successfully during TEST B — the parent's roster reverted cleanly to [amu, john, QAKid1784673895] after TEST B and reload.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Delete-of-just-added child (TEST B) now works locally AND on manager. Delete-of-pre-existing-child (TEST C) is STILL BROKEN — QAKid1784673895 revives on parent reload and continues to appear on manager Members. Root cause: the Supabase pushMemberRosterContribution is failing ('TypeError: Failed to fetch' logged on every Save) so remote holds a stale 3-child contribution; on parent reload the stale remote (or auth.user_metadata) re-injects QAKid. When main agent fixes: (1) add supabase.auth.updateUser({data:{registeredChildren:next}}) inside handleSaveChildren for both add AND delete; (2) add a monotonic client updated_at and prefer local when local is fresher; (3) retry the contribution push on failure. Then re-run TEST C: as parent, delete QAKid + Save + reload => kids should be [amu, john]; manager Members should be 'Showing 2 of 2 members' with QAKid absent within ~15s. Also please cross-check that Supabase from the preview browser is actually reachable (all pushes showed Failed to fetch this run) — it may be a preview-env network issue that hides the durability of the fix.
rca_of_the_issue: REPRODUCTION (TEST C, confirmed twice): 1) Login parent cooler74.ea@gmail.com / test12. 2) Baseline dlbc_players parent.registeredChildren = [amu, john, QAKid1784673895]. 3) Open Manage children, click Remove on the QAKid card (verified: QAKid input count 1 -> 0 in the DOM). 4) Click Save children. 5) Immediately localStorage.dlbc_players parent.registeredChildren = [amu, john] (delete applied locally, no error surfaced to UI). Console warning: '[app_state] member contribution sync failed TypeError: Failed to fetch' — the Supabase push failed. 6) page.reload(). 7) After reload, localStorage.dlbc_players parent.registeredChildren = [amu, john, QAKid1784673895] — QAKid REAPPEARS. 8) Login manager -> Members: Showing 3 of 3, QAKid still listed. HYPOTHESIS: Because the Supabase pushMemberRosterContribution fails (network Failed to fetch), the remote dlbc_roster_contrib:<uid> row (or auth.user_metadata.registeredChildren) still holds all 3 children. On next mount, pullRemoteAppState + mergeContributionIntoPlayers OR syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata rehydrates the deleted child before the fresh local state can 'win'. The 250ms debounce fixes intra-session ordering but does nothing for network-failed pushes. TEST B accidentally passes for a JUST-ADDED child only because that child was never successfully synced to remote either — remote had no knowledge of it, so on reload the local state (missing the child) wins by default. MITIGATION: (a) always mirror the shrunken registeredChildren into supabase.auth.updateUser (auth is a different Supabase endpoint; if that too fails add a retry queue); (b) tag every dlbc_players write with a monotonic client updated_at and make the merge routines prefer whichever side is newer, not 'local wins only when empty'; (c) retry pushMemberRosterContribution with exponential backoff on network failures; (d) verify Supabase reachability from the preview browser — Failed to fetch may indicate a wider integration issue that was masked by the localStorage-only success path in TEST B.
